# `pcie_dma_engine` вЂ” PCIe Gen2 Г—4 DMA Engine

> Part of [SYSTEM.md](SYSTEM.md) В· **Instantiated by**: [`kc705_top`](kc705_top.md)

## Purpose

Transfers the 128-byte BBO snapshot from `snapshot_mux` to host DRAM via PCIe Gen2 Г—4 using a descriptor-ring DMA model. Runs every 10 ms. The Vivado IP `pcie_7x_0` (hard PCIe endpoint) must be generated in the project before synthesis; it is not included in the repository.

## Ports

### Snapshot Interface (from `lliu_top_v2` / `snapshot_mux`)

| Port | Dir | Width | Description |
|------|-----|-------|-------------|
| `snap_req` | out | 1 | One-cycle pulse: request new snapshot (in `sys_clk` domain) |
| `snap_data` | in | 64 | Snapshot beat (registered in `pcie_dma_engine` from combinational source) |
| `snap_valid` | in | 1 | Snapshot beat valid |
| `snap_ready` | out | 1 | AXI4-S style ready |
| `snap_done` | in | 1 | One-cycle pulse: all 128 bytes received |

### AXI4-Lite BAR0 Slave (host-facing configuration)

| Address | Register | Access |
|---------|----------|--------|
| `0x000` | `CTRL` вЂ” bit[0] = DMA enable | RW |
| `0x004` | `STATUS` вЂ” bit[0] = DMA busy, bit[1] = descriptor ring full | RO |
| `0x008` | `DESC_WR_PTR` вЂ” host write pointer into descriptor ring | WO |
| `0x00C` | `DESC_HOST_LO` вЂ” host buffer physical address [31:0] | WO |
| `0x010` | `DESC_HOST_HI` вЂ” host buffer physical address [63:32] | WO |
| `0x014` | `DESC_LEN` вЂ” byte count for next descriptor | WO |

### PCIe (Vivado IP)

| Port | Dir | Width | Description |
|------|-----|-------|-------------|
| `pcie_txp/txn` | out | 4 | PCIe transmit differential pair |
| `pcie_rxp/rxn` | in | 4 | PCIe receive differential pair |
| `pcie_refclk` | in | 1 | 100 MHz PCIe reference clock |
| `pcie_rst_n` | in | 1 | PCIe reset (active low) |

## Clock Domains

| Domain | Frequency | Source |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| `sys_clk` | 312.5 MHz | Main inference clock |
| `user_clk` | 250 MHz | PCIe IP user clock (generated by `pcie_7x_0`) |

Two-flop CDC synchronizers are used for `snap_req` (sys_clk в†’ user_clk) and `snap_done` (sys_clk в†’ user_clk). The snapshot data itself is small enough (128 bytes) to be buffered in a shallow synchronous FIFO before the TLP packer.

## Descriptor Ring

- **RAMB36E1**, 256 entries Г— 96-bit wide.
- Entry format: `{host_addr[63:0], byte_len[23:0], valid[0], flags[7:0]}`.
- Host writes descriptors via BAR0; hardware increments a read pointer after each successful DMA.
- Ring full condition: `(wr_ptr + 1) % 256 == rd_ptr`.

## DMA Operation (10 ms interval)

1. A 10 ms timer (counter at `user_clk` frequency) fires `snap_req` (via CDC to `sys_clk`).
2. `snapshot_mux` streams 128 bytes (16 Г— 8-byte beats) to the FIFO.
3. TLP packer assembles 64-bit-addressed Memory Write TLPs, 128 bytes per TLP (to fill 4DW payload, MRRS-limited).
4. A single TLP covers the 128-byte snapshot. Host DMA descriptor is consumed.
5. `STATUS.busy` de-asserts after TLP completion.

## TLP Details

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Format | 3DW header + data (64-bit address = 4DW header for >4 GB) |
| Type | Memory Write |
| Payload | 128 bytes (32 DW) per TLP |
| Length | 0x020 (32 DW) |
| Traffic class | 0 |

## Timing

- `sys_clk`: 312.5 MHz; `user_clk`: 250 MHz.
- CDC synchronizer latency: 2 cycles in the destination domain.
- PCIe IP must be configured for Gen2 Г—4 (5 GT/s, 4 lanes) in Vivado.
- The `pcie_7x_0` IP block uses the hard PCIe block in the Kintex-7 silicon; it is not available in the free Vivado ML Standard license for device families that require it as an encrypted IP вЂ” verify license availability before synthesis.
